jokes.text: Hey, wouldn't it be cool if our duck and our chick had a little baby? We could call it Chuck.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting a name for a hypothetical baby between their duck and chick
jokes.punchline: Chuck - a portmanteau of 'chick' and 'duck'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Joey's childlike enthusiasm. Clean execution but predictable.

jokes.text: Or Dick.
jokes.setup: Following Joey's 'Chuck' suggestion
jokes.punchline: Dick - cruder portmanteau that escalates the joke
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler escalation - takes Joey's innocent joke and makes it slightly inappropriate with maximum economy.

jokes.text: Was it like a sneeze, only better?
jokes.setup: Ross describing finding 'something' while cleansing himself in the shower
jokes.punchline: Joey's sexual interpretation of Ross's innocent medical concern
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding - his innocent sexual confusion creates perfect misdirection from Ross's actual medical concern.

jokes.text: Whoa! Whoa! No. / Come on, you guys. It's no big deal. / Hey, hey, hey.
jokes.setup: Ross asking his friends to look at something on his body
jokes.punchline: Chandler and Joey's horrified physical recoil vs Ross's casual attitude
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy reaction. Well-executed but predictable male friendship dynamics.

jokes.text: No, it's too wrinkly to be a mole.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Ross's thing is a mole
jokes.punchline: Joey's uncomfortably specific visual description
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works through specificity. Joey's willingness to examine it closely adds character.

jokes.text: No, it's... fancier than a pimple.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting it might be a pimple
jokes.punchline: Ross describing a skin abnormality as 'fancy'
jokes.explanation: Excellent word choice - 'fancier' is an absurd way to describe a growth. Shows Ross's intellectual vocabulary applied inappropriately.

jokes.text: Okay, well, it's definite. Two more weeks of winter.
jokes.setup: Rachel entering mid-examination of Ross's body issue
jokes.punchline: Groundhog Day reference suggesting Ross is like a groundhog seeing his shadow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual callback to Groundhog Day that perfectly captures the absurdity of the situation. Economy and timing are perfect.

jokes.text: Well, you know, if Joey and I play with matches... we could get you up to an even 100.
jokes.setup: Vince mentioning his 98 hot saves as a fireman
jokes.punchline: Chandler offering to create fires for Vince to rescue them from
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - helpful in the most inappropriate way possible. Dark humor with perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Fire safety is not a joke, son.
jokes.setup: Chandler's joke about starting fires
jokes.punchline: Vince's overly serious, fatherly response to obvious joke
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - shows Vince is humorless about his job. The 'son' adds patronizing touch.

jokes.text: You know, this kind of, like, you know, oat-sowing, field-playing juggler.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing herself as playing the field and sowing wild oats
jokes.punchline: Her convoluted compound description that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe verbal confusion - overthinks common phrases into meaningless word salad. Perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Does a dog's lips move when he reads?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if the two guys know about each other
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's nonsensical rhetorical question instead of just saying 'no'
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Phoebe absurdity. Completely unexpected response that somehow makes perfect sense for her character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Because when I went in there with my third nipple... he just lopped it right off, you know?
jokes.setup: Chandler recommending his doctor to Ross
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of his own bizarre body abnormality
jokes.explanation: Great character reveal delivered casually. Establishes running gag about Chandler's body issues with perfect nonchalance.

jokes.text: I mean, not as lucky as people who are born with two nipples.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he's lucky about his third nipple being removed
jokes.punchline: His acknowledgment that normal people are actually luckier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler self-deprecation. The mock-philosophical tone about nipple quantity is absurd and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Oh. Maybe they'll name yours after you. You know? They'd call it The Ross.
jokes.setup: Chandler noting that at least his condition had a name
jokes.punchline: Joey's idea that Ross's mystery growth could become a medical eponym
jokes.explanation: Joey's optimistic spin on Ross's medical mystery. The specificity of 'The Ross' makes it funnier than generic naming.

jokes.text: And then people would be, like, 'Oh, he's got a Ross.'
jokes.setup: Joey's suggestion about naming the condition 'The Ross'
jokes.punchline: Joey imagining future casual usage of Ross's name as a medical term
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the naming joke. Joey's ability to imagine the future colloquial usage adds layers.

jokes.text: Yeah, that'd be cool.
jokes.setup: Joey's elaborate fantasy about Ross's condition becoming famous
jokes.punchline: Ross's understated positive reaction to having a gross medical condition named after him
jokes.explanation: Ross's nerdy pride in potential medical fame. Perfect character beat - he'd genuinely like the academic recognition.

jokes.text: Yeah. Maybe he just cheated on you.
jokes.setup: Monica being told 'we need to talk' doesn't necessarily mean breakup
jokes.punchline: Phoebe offering an even worse alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - tries to be helpful but makes it worse. Great subversion of expected comfort.

jokes.text: We don't need a fireman. We need, like, a good mechanic.
jokes.setup: Fire department arriving to help with what seems like an emergency
jokes.punchline: Phoebe suggesting they need a mechanic instead, implying the 'fire' is mechanical
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection about the nature of their problem. Phoebe's practical problem-solving creates unexpected humor.

jokes.text: Look, if I wanted to see a fireman, I would date one, okay?
jokes.setup: Phoebe avoiding the arriving firemen
jokes.punchline: Her reason for avoiding them, not knowing the audience knows she IS dating one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - audience knows she IS dating a fireman. Perfect setup for her awkward situation escalation.

jokes.text: First of all, it's on your ass.
jokes.setup: Chandler showing what he thinks is his third nipple
jokes.punchline: Doctor's blunt anatomical correction
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Chandler's casual medical knowledge. The doctor's matter-of-fact tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Or, ha, ha... we could go over there and pee on them.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Joey offering to 'water' Pete's plants if he breaks up with Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler's clarification that he meant literally urinating on them
jokes.explanation: Juvenile escalation that feels somewhat cheap. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, it doesn't feel like playing anymore. It's feels like work. It's like I'm working in the field.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she doesn't want to 'play the field' anymore
jokes.punchline: Her literal interpretation of the metaphor - working in an actual field
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe literal interpretation of metaphor. Shows her unique thought process while being genuinely funny.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. I meant Chandler.
jokes.setup: Rachel calling Jason a sissy after Chandler suggests going with the sensitive guy
jokes.punchline: Rachel clarifying she was calling Chandler the sissy, not Jason
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that recontextualizes the insult. Sharp timing and unexpected target make it effective.

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, I've been practicing medicine for 23 years... and I'm stumped.
jokes.setup: Multiple doctors examining Ross's mysterious growth
jokes.punchline: Experienced doctor's dramatic admission of complete bafflement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the medical mystery. The formal announcement format adds absurdity to Ross's embarrassing situation.

jokes.text: Whoa. For a rich guy, that's a pretty small TV.
jokes.setup: Joey observing Pete's apartment
jokes.punchline: Joey's disappointment that wealth doesn't guarantee large TVs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - judges wealth by TV size. Shows his working-class values and simple materialism.

jokes.text: The refrigerator told me to have a great day.
jokes.setup: Characters admiring Pete's high-tech kitchen
jokes.punchline: Chandler's discovery of an overly friendly smart appliance
jokes.explanation: Great tech humor for the era. The anthropomorphizing of appliances hits the sweet spot of futuristic absurdity.

jokes.text: Yeah, I see him. You guys are just the worst hiders ever.
jokes.setup: Monica's friends hiding during videophone call
jokes.punchline: Pete casually pointing out he can see them all clearly
jokes.explanation: Solid deflation of their hiding attempt. Pete's matter-of-fact delivery makes their childish behavior funnier.

jokes.text: We love you too.
jokes.setup: Pete telling Monica he loves her over videophone
jokes.punchline: All the friends responding in unison as if he was talking to them
jokes.explanation: Great awkward group moment. The automatic response shows how close-knit they are while being appropriately embarrassing.

jokes.text: And that's Pete's mom. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to call Monica's mom about the potential engagement
jokes.punchline: Joey accidentally calling Pete's mother instead
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken identity gag. Joey's sheepish admission is in character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I mean, it'll be dry, but people will like it.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting a money-themed wedding with money salad
jokes.punchline: Her acknowledgment that money salad would taste terrible but guests would enjoy it anyway
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation. The practical concern about taste in an impractical scenario adds humor layers.

jokes.text: Oh, what do you know? You married a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Ross giving relationship advice about not rushing into marriage
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out Ross's notably poor track record with marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character moment. Joey's blunt dismissal of Ross's advice based on concrete evidence is devastatingly effective.

jokes.text: ...just, like, a really, really, really nice body.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she chose Jason over Vince for his sensitivity
jokes.punchline: Her admission that she's giving up Vince's extremely attractive physique
jokes.explanation: The escalation of 'really's shows Phoebe's genuine struggle with the physical attraction vs. emotional connection dilemma.

jokes.text: Sorry. I was just imagining what it'd be like to catch the money bouquet.
jokes.setup: Rachel getting caught up in Monica's potential wedding plans
jokes.punchline: Her visualization of a money bouquet instead of flowers
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the money wedding theme. Rachel's materialistic fantasy is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go write in my journal.
jokes.setup: Vince appearing to be a stereotypical tough fireman
jokes.punchline: His sensitive response to emotional pain involving journaling
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion. The tough fireman having a journal flips expectations and creates Phoebe's dilemma.

jokes.text: Thank you, but I wanna remove it, Pheebs. I don't wanna make it savory.
jokes.setup: Phoebe recommending her herbal guy for Ross's medical issue
jokes.punchline: Ross's interpretation that herbs would make his growth taste better
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on herbal remedies. Ross's academic precision applied to grossout humor works perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, for clubbing.
jokes.setup: Looking at an expensive wedding dress that Monica should get anyway
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggesting she wear a wedding dress to go clubbing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's fashion-forward thinking, even if impractical. The casualness sells it.

jokes.text: He'd prefer watercolors, but, you know, he has easy access to a lot of charcoal.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing Vince's artwork
jokes.punchline: The implication that he gets charcoal from fire scenes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - suggesting he sources art supplies from tragedy. Phoebe's matter-of-fact delivery makes it perfect.

jokes.text: So... It's really just about the math.
jokes.setup: Phoebe weighing her two romantic options with various criteria
jokes.punchline: Reducing her romantic decision to a mathematical calculation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - turning romance into arithmetic. The deadpan delivery of such an unromantic conclusion is excellent.

jokes.text: I don't know. What's a kundus with you?
jokes.setup: Ross asking what a 'kundus' is
jokes.punchline: Guru making it into a 'What's up with you?' pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but feels somewhat forced. The guru character setup supports it but it's not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We appear to have angered it.
jokes.setup: Something going wrong during Ross's treatment
jokes.punchline: Treating the growth like an angry living creature
jokes.explanation: Great anthropomorphization of Ross's medical condition. The plural 'we' suggests shared responsibility for upsetting it.

jokes.text: Got caught in my watch.
jokes.setup: Guru building up mysterious treatment with 'love' as the solution
jokes.punchline: The growth accidentally being removed by getting snagged on his watch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anticlimax. All the medical expertise and mystical treatment defeated by accidental snagging. Perfect deflation.

jokes.text: Monica? I wanna become... the Ultimate Fighting Champion.
jokes.setup: Pete building up to romantic proposal with talk of conquering worlds
jokes.punchline: Revealing his desire is for violent combat sports, not marriage
jokes.explanation: Masterful misdirection - audience expects proposal, gets combat sports. The setup is perfect, payoff is shocking and hilarious.

jokes.text: I want you close enough to smell the blood.
jokes.setup: Pete wanting Monica at his fights
jokes.punchline: His romantic description involving the smell of blood
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation. Pete's attempt at romantic imagery using violent details is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: My parents will be so happy.
jokes.setup: Pete's announcement about wanting to be a violent fighter
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic comment about her parents' likely reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica reaction - practical concern about family approval in absurd situation. Deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I don't know. He just started kissing me. Get him, Vince.
jokes.setup: Phoebe caught between her two boyfriends
jokes.punchline: Her immediate attempt to turn them against each other instead of explaining
jokes.explanation: Good panic response from Phoebe - trying to deflect rather than take responsibility. In character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: And it's been really horrible, because, you know, it's been... a lot of fun for me.
jokes.setup: Phoebe confessing that dating both has been horrible
jokes.punchline: Her admission that it's actually been fun, contradicting herself
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment - her honesty undermining her attempt at appearing remorseful. Self-contradictory in a perfectly characteristic way.

jokes.text: You know, I can't believe I went out with somebody... who'd actually have an open flame in the middle of a wooded area.
jokes.setup: Vince losing Phoebe to Jason
jokes.punchline: Fireman's professional disapproval of Jason's fire safety violation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character consistency - Vince can't get past the fire hazard even when heartbroken. Professional priorities over romantic ones.

jokes.text: Hello. I am Guru Saj.
jokes.setup: Duck coughing, needing medical attention
jokes.punchline: The same questionable guru now treating animals
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the guru's questionable credentials extend to veterinary care. Sets up final absurd moment.

jokes.text: Do you think you could get him to eat a bat?
jokes.setup: Guru examining the sick duck
jokes.punchline: His bizarre treatment recommendation involving bat consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd ending. The casual suggestion of interspecies consumption as medical treatment is perfectly weird for this character.
